skill.name: network-engineer
skill.description: Expert network engineer specializing in modern cloud networking, security architectures, and performance optimization. Masters multi-cloud connectivity, service mesh, zero-trust networking, SSL/TLS, global load balancing, and advanced troubleshooting. Handles CDN optimization, network automation, and compliance. Use PROACTIVELY for network design, connectivity issues, or performance optimization.
skill.summary: Plan, secure, troubleshoot, and optimize modern cloud and hybrid networks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backticks and weak cryptographic algorithms are false positives from Markdown text and words such as SSL/TLS, considers, and design. The line 94 nmap and packet-analysis references are legitimate network engineering content but are dual-use, so publication should include a network-use warning. No prompt injection, credential access, executable code, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: Dual-Use Network Scanning Tool Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 94 lists diagnostic tools including nmap, tcpdump, Wireshark, mtr, and iperf3. These tools are common for authorized troubleshooting, but they can also support reconnaissance or scanning if used without permission.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tool names are present in a troubleshooting section, which supports legitimate intent. The same tools are clearly dual-use, so a medium-risk warning is appropriate.

low_findings.title: Static Keyword Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static findings at lines 3, 7, 28, 41, 135, 152, 162, and 166 are prose-only matches. Line 28 is an inline Markdown path, and the other lines do not provide executable commands, weak cryptographic configuration, or reconnaissance instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible prose and Markdown, not code. No command execution, weak algorithm selection, or malicious instruction is present at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Reconnaissance Tooling
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references nmap and packet-analysis tools in a diagnostic context. This is appropriate for authorized network engineering but can be misused against networks without consent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The location directly names network analysis and scanning tools. The surrounding section frames them as troubleshooting utilities, which lowers the concern from high to medium.

content.value_statement: Cloud and hybrid networks are hard to design, secure, and troubleshoot across providers. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured network engineering guidance.
actual_capabilities: Designs AWS, Azure, GCP, hybrid, and multi-cloud network architectures.
Explains load balancing, DNS, CDN, SSL/TLS, PKI, and service mesh patterns.
Guides authorized troubleshooting across DNS, transport, application, and cloud network layers.
Recommends zero-trust controls, segmentation, VPNs, firewall rules, and monitoring plans.
Helps document topology, capacity plans, failover paths, and operational runbooks.
limitations: Does not directly access live networks, cloud consoles, packet captures, or monitoring systems.
Requires accurate topology, configuration, logs, and ownership context from the user.
Network scanning advice must be used only on systems the user is authorized to test.
Does not replace vendor support for outages, managed service incidents, or regulatory sign-off.

use_cases.title: Plan Multi-Cloud Connectivity
use_cases.description: Create a secure routing, VPN, peering, and failover design across cloud providers.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot Service Connectivity
use_cases.description: Work through DNS, routing, firewall, load balancer, and service mesh checks in order.

use_cases.title: Harden Network Security
use_cases.description: Review segmentation, zero-trust access, certificate handling, and monitoring controls.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Network Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a network engineer. Help me troubleshoot this connectivity issue. Ask for missing details, then give a layered checklist for DNS, routing, firewall, load balancer, and application checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Cloud Network
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a secure cloud network for this workload. Include subnet layout, routing, ingress, egress, private connectivity, DNS, monitoring, and failover recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Global Delivery
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this global application delivery plan. Recommend CDN, DNS, load balancing, TLS, caching, observability, and capacity improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Zero-Trust Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this network architecture for zero-trust readiness. Identify segmentation gaps, identity controls, certificate requirements, logging needs, and migration steps.

output_examples.input: A Kubernetes service intermittently fails between namespaces.
output: A layered troubleshooting plan for service discovery, network policies, ingress, service mesh routing, and pod-level connectivity.
A short list of likely causes and the evidence needed to confirm each one.

output_examples.input: A team needs active-active traffic across two cloud regions.
output: A high-level architecture covering global load balancing, health checks, DNS behavior, private connectivity, and failover testing.
Operational notes for monitoring latency, packet loss, and regional capacity.

output_examples.input: A company needs better TLS and certificate operations.
output: A certificate lifecycle plan with issuance, renewal, validation, alerting, and incident response steps.
Security recommendations for protocol versions, cipher policy, and trust boundaries.

best_practices: Provide topology, provider, region, routing, firewall, DNS, and recent change details before asking for analysis.
Use diagnostic or scanning guidance only on networks where you have explicit authorization.
Validate recommendations in a staging environment before changing production routing or security controls.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad scanning of unknown public networks or third-party systems.
Do not apply network changes without a rollback plan and maintenance window.
Do not share private keys, live credentials, or sensitive packet data in prompts.

faq.question: Can this skill configure my cloud network directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides design, troubleshooting, and review guidance. You must apply changes through your approved tools.

faq.question: Does it support AWS, Azure, and GCP?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers common networking services and patterns across AWS, Azure, GCP, hybrid, and multi-cloud environments.

faq.question: Can it help during an outage?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure triage steps and evidence collection, but it cannot inspect live systems unless you provide data.

faq.question: Is network scanning allowed with this skill?
faq.answer: Only authorized scanning is appropriate. Use diagnostic tool guidance on systems you own or have permission to test.

faq.question: Can it review zero-trust designs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review segmentation, identity controls, mTLS, policy enforcement, logging, and migration priorities.

faq.question: Will it produce compliance certification?
faq.answer: No. It can help prepare evidence and improve controls, but formal compliance requires qualified review.
